facilities.boys_hostel: 350 capacity, Warden: Dr. Srinivasa M R +919448282593
facilities.sports: Cricket stadium, Swimming pool, Tennis courts

required_documents: CET/COMED-K Allotment Order and Rank Card

Admission.question: How do I get admission?
Admission.answer: Admission is processed through CET, COMED-K, or Management quota (UNAIDED). You must attend the respective counseling sessions after clearing the entrance exams and select PESCE.

Admission.question: What are admission codes?
Admission.answer: Use code E023 for Karnataka CET, E058 for UNAIDED (Management), and E089 for COMED-K during your option entry.

Admission.question: What documents do I need?
Admission.answer: You need your 10th and 12th marks cards, Transfer Certificate, Entrance Exam Rank Card & Allotment Order, Aadhaar copy, Caste/Income certificate (if applicable), and 4 passport size photos.

Admission.question: What's the fee structure?
Admission.answer: Fees vary strictly by quota. CET students pay government-subsidized fees, COMED-K students pay standard private fees, and UNAIDED (Management) varies by branch.

Admission.question: Can I apply for multiple admission types?
Admission.answer: Yes, you can write multiple entrance exams (like CET and COMED-K) but you can only lock the seat under a single quota at the time of admission.

Academic.question: How many years is the course?
Academic.answer: The standard B.E. (Bachelor of Engineering) program strictly lasts for 4 years, divided into 8 semesters.

Academic.question: What's the difference between CSE and CSE(AI&ML)?
Academic.answer: CSE is general computer science covering all software engineering fundamentals. CSE(AI&ML) specializes heavily in artificial intelligence, neural networks, and data science earlier in the curriculum.

Academic.question: Is the college autonomous?
Academic.answer: Yes! PESCE has been an autonomous institution under VTU since 2008, meaning we design our own advanced curriculum and conduct our own exams.

Academic.question: What's the exam pattern?
Academic.answer: We follow a 50-50 model: Continuous Internal Evaluation (CIE) makes up 50 marks, and the Semester End Examination (SEE) makes up the remaining 50 marks.

Academic.question: Can I change my branch?
Academic.answer: Branch changes are permitted at the start of the 3rd semester (2nd year) but are strictly dependent on your 1st-year CGPA and seat availability in the desired branch.

Placement.question: What's the average package?
Placement.answer: The average package highly depends on the branch, but generally ranges between 5 to 8 LPA, with top technical roles exceeding 12-15+ LPA.

Placement.question: Which companies visit?
Placement.answer: Over 350 companies visit, including giants like TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Accenture, Capgemini, HCL, IBM, Deloitte, Cisco, and Mercedes Benz.

Placement.question: What's the placement percentage?
Placement.answer: Historically, placement percentages have remained robust across eligible students, often exceeding 85-90% for core IT and CSE branches.

Placement.question: Are internships available?
Placement.answer: Yes, mandatory internships are built into the curriculum. Many top companies also offer pre-placement internships during the 7th/8th semester.

Placement.question: When does placement season start?
Placement.answer: Campus placement drives primarily kick off at the very beginning of the 7th semester (final year).

Hostel.question: Are hostels mandatory?
Hostel.answer: No, staying in the college hostel is completely optional. Day scholars commute normally.

Hostel.question: What's the cost?
Hostel.answer: Hostel costs combine an annual room rent along with monthly sliding mess bills depending on the food divided among students. Contact the wardens for precise current rates.

Hostel.question: What facilities are included?
Hostel.answer: Hostels include 24/7 security, medical emergency support via our dispensary, standard mess food (North & South Indian), and basic furniture.

Hostel.question: Can I get outside accommodation?
Hostel.answer: Yes, many PG (Paying Guest) accommodations and rental options exist around the Mandya campus within walking distance.

Hostel.question: Who's the hostel warden?
Hostel.answer: For Boys: Dr. Srinivasa M R (+91 94482 82593). For Girls: Shwetha M K (+91 94482 82595).

General.question: How do I contact college?
General.answer: You can reach the main admission line at +91 94482 82588 or email admissions@pesce.ac.in.

General.question: Where is the campus?
General.answer: PES College of Engineering is completely situated in Mandya, Karnataka. It's easily accessible via the Bangalore-Mysore highway.

General.question: Are there sports facilities?
General.answer: Yes! The campus boasts a massive cricket stadium, tennis courts, and a swimming pool.

General.question: Is WiFi available?
General.answer: Yes, Wi-Fi is actively available across academic blocks, the central library, and the hostel premises.
